metric_value: 10-100x performance improvement for in-memory database operations over non-VLM systems
notes: Aberdeen cites 1-2 orders of magnitude improvement for cost of adding main memory

metric_value: ADABAS D V6 supports 10-gigabytes-and-over data caches
notes: Exceptional for 1996; commodity servers had 256MB-1GB RAM maximum

metric_value: Upwards of $500 million in revenues in fiscal 1995
notes: Large supplier for data management; primarily US data centers and European market

metric_value: More than 15,000 Alpha servers shipped in the past year
notes: Digital indicates strong Alpha momentum; also has large VAX installed base upgrading

metric_value: 32-bit architectures address less than 1GB main memory; 64-bit Alpha increases addressable memory by several orders of magnitude
notes: Fundamental architectural constraint of 1990s computing; 64-bit addressing the solution

metric_value: VLM costs less than adding another system or solid-state disk; boosts topped-out SMP systems
notes: Economic argument for VLM adoption vs alternative performance scaling approaches

metric_value: Available on HP-UX, IBM AIX, Sun Solaris, SCO Unix, Digital Unix, Windows NT, OS/2; broad cross-platform strategy
notes: Multi-platform availability as competitive differentiator

metric_value: Aberdeen implies DEC has strong market position via Alpha; large VAX install base upgrading to Alpha
notes: Aberdeen did not explicitly predict DEC viability but recommended the combination

metric_value: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998; largest computer industry merger at the time
notes: DEC was sold; Compaq then acquired by HP in 2002; Alpha architecture discontinued

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends IS buyers consider Software AG/Digital for RDBMS and VLM deployments; implies long-term viability
notes: Implied prediction of Software AG ADABAS D survival

metric_value: Software AG survived and grew; acquired by Silver Lake in 2023 for €2.2B; ADABAS & Natural being spun off as independent business in 2025
notes: ADABAS has survived 50+ years; still has active installed base in mainframe/legacy environments

metric_value: VLM technology extends high-performance database to broader enterprise applications; orders-of-magnitude improvements achievable
notes: Aberdeen predicts in-memory computing will transform enterprise data management

metric_value: In-memory databases (SAP HANA 2011, Redis, Memcached, VoltDB) became mainstream; Aberdeen's directional prediction proved accurate
notes: Aberdeen correctly identified the trajectory; specific technology vendors changed dramatically

metric_value: Near-lights-out automated distributed-database administration; lower cost of ownership than Oracle/DB2
notes: Key selling point vs Oracle for enterprise deployments

metric_value: ADABAS D integrates with mainframe Oracle, ADABAS C, and DB2 via ENTIRE networking
notes: Critical differentiator for enterprises with mainframe investment

metric_value: Available on HP-UX, AIX, Sun Solaris, SCO Unix, Digital Unix, Windows NT, OS/2
notes: Broad platform support strategy; broader than most RDBMS vendors at the time

metric_name: VAX to Alpha Migration Installed Base
metric_value: Digital has large installed VAX customer base currently upgrading to Alpha
notes: Migration opportunity as both growth driver and installed base risk

metric_value: VLM enables ultra-high-performance OLTP by keeping hot data in main memory
notes: First of four key VLM use cases identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: VLM enables effective end-user access to larger databases with complex queries
notes: Data mart use case for VLM

metric_value: VLM delivers competitive advantage for ERP including SAP R/3 at medium enterprise scale
notes: SAP R/3 on Alpha/ADABAS D demonstrated in customer sites
